market_context: Markets opened down 1-2% after Trump announced Iran port blockade following failed peace talks. Oil surged 8% to $103+ while Goldman Sachs beat earnings but fell on weak trading. High volatility expected.

predictions.reasoning: Pre-market futures down 1.1% on Iran blockade news and oil price surge. Energy inflation concerns likely to weigh on broad market sentiment despite strong earnings season start.
Energy Select Sector SPDR should benefit directly from 8% oil price surge and supply disruption fears. Strait of Hormuz blockade threatens 20% of global oil transit.
Despite beating earnings with $17.55 EPS vs $16.49 expected, shares already down 3% pre-market on disappointing FICC trading results. Geopolitical uncertainty adds pressure.
Financial sector faces headwinds from broader market selloff and Goldman's disappointing trading results setting negative tone ahead of JPM/WFC earnings Tuesday.
Flight to quality trade likely as geopolitical tensions escalate and oil-driven inflation concerns may delay Fed cuts. Long-term treasuries should benefit from safe haven demand.
